name: Risotto Di Zucca
recipeInstructions:
2 1/4 pounds, or about 3-4 cups cubed. If kabocha squash is unavailable, use buttercup squash, one of the hubbards, or a couple of acorn squashes. Avoid blander varieties such as butternut and banana squashes; choose ones with rich, sweet flavor.
1. Peel onions and cut into quarters, then slice crosswise.
2. Peel Kabocha squash, discard dark-green parts and seeds, then cut into 1/2-inch cubes.
3. Heat oil and butter in a large, preferably non-stick saute pan.
4. Add onions and garlic; stir over high heat for 3-4 minutes.
5. Add cubed squash, salt, and sage. Continue cooking on medium-high heat, stirring often until vegetables are golden brown and squash is tender—about 15 minutes.
6. Meanwhile, bring vegetable broth to a simmer.
7. Add rice to the squash mixture; stir constantly for 2 minutes.
8. Quickly add Marsala, then white wine; cook away the alcohol.
9. When wine is absorbed, add about one cup of hot broth, along with balsamic vinegar. Lower heat to a simmer and gently stir risotto with a wooden spoon.
10. As broth is absorbed, continue adding ladles until rice is al dente, tender yet firm, with a creamy sauce—about 30 minutes total. Taste after 20 minutes.
11. Adjust seasoning with salt and pepper if needed, then stir in grated Parmesan cheese. Add another ladle of broth, stir well, and serve immediately in warmed shallow bowls. Garnish each serving with slivers of fresh basil; pass additional Parmesan cheese separately.
Source: "The New Vegetarian Epicure" by Anna Thomas
